name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-29
title: Wave-2 deferred: invariant numbering monotonic-order reorder + enforcement test
description:
Deferred from wave-1 housekeeping (separate from BACKLOG-25 which is about duplicate numbering; this is about non-monotonic ORDERING).

Spec.md has invariants in non-sequential document order:
- Inv 86, 87 (added by wave-1) appear inline among Inv 53-60.
- Inv 88, 89 (added by BACKLOG-27 in wave-1) sit between Inv 59 and 60.
- Numerically-out-of-order blocks create reader confusion: scanning sequentially produces 53 -> 86 -> 87 -> 54 -> 55 -> 88 -> 89 -> 56...

This is structural debt that compounds with each future invariant addition (new invariants get appended wherever they conceptually fit, not where their number sequences).

Resolution: one-pass renumber of all invariants in spec.md so document order matches numeric order monotonically. Then add a new contract-feature enforcement: 'check-invariant-monotonic-order.py' (or extend check-numbered-lists.py) that walks each feature's spec.md and verifies invariant numbers appear in monotonically-increasing order. Wire into contract/test/run.py.

This invariant could become a project-wide rule (file under contract). All features benefit.

Cross-feature consideration: rabbit-feature has the same problem (Inv 35 appears before Inv 33/34 in document order — tracked separately in RABBIT-FEATURE-BACKLOG-X). The new enforcement test would catch both at once.

Acceptance: rabbit-cage spec.md invariants in monotonic document order; new enforcement test exists and passes for every feature; reference numbers in tests/code updated where renumbering changed any invariant identity.

history.note: Reopening to re-close with explanatory reason per wave-2 orchestration decision (overrides default auto-close from TDD subagent).

Spec renumber complete (this cycle's impl commit). Enforcement test (check-invariant-monotonic-order.py) moved to CONTRACT-BACKLOG-30 scope per wave-2 orchestration decision; folded into that cycle to land alongside contract's other test/spec cleanup.
